content:
{
  "title": "Self-Contrastive Meta-Prompting for Multilingual LLMs in Low-Resource Settings",
  "problem": "Existing prompting methods often fail to robustly guide large language models (LLMs) in multilingual or low-resource language tasks due to insufficient context sensitivity and transferability across languages. The specific challenge is to design prompts that dynamically adapt to language-specific features while leveraging shared knowledge from high-resource languages.",
  "motivation": "Performance gaps between high-resource and low-resource languages persist in LLMs, limiting their utility for cross-lingual tasks and inclusion of underrepresented languages. There is a pressing need for prompting strategies that can automatically adapt to language-specific idiosyncrasies and data scarcity, especially as multilingual LLMs become widely deployed. A method that can meta-learn and self-adjust prompts for any language can push the boundaries of cross-lingual NLP.",
  "method": "We propose a Self-Contrastive Meta-Prompting framework, where the LLM is prompted with a set of language-agnostic meta-instructions and a pair of few-shot language-specific exemplars: one from a high-resource language, one from the target low-resource language. The prompt instructs the model to explicitly compare and contrast the exemplars and self-modify its behavior to maximize alignment with both linguistic patterns. This self-contrastive meta-prompt triggers the model to internally calibrate style, syntax, and semantics, allowing for zero-shot and few-shot adaptation to low-resource tasks. The approach is training-free and operates entirely at inference time.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on multilingual classification and generation benchmarks (e.g., XTREME, FLORES-200) focusing on low-resource subsets. Baselines include standard direct prompting, chain-of-thought, and translation-based prompts. Metrics are accuracy, BLEU, and cross-lingual transfer efficiency. Ablation studies will assess the contribution of self-contrastive and meta-prompt components."
}
